items.headline: Campbell increases business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance revising business license tax rates in the Campbell Municipal Code. This annual update affects local businesses operating in the city.

items.headline: Council Defers Affordable Housing Affordability Restrictions Decision
items.summary: A decision on how the city handles affordability restrictions when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure was postponed to April 28. This affects long-term affordable housing stability in San José.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves giant stadium signage
items.summary: The City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. This allows the city to display large promotional graphics in the stadium vicinity for the limited event period.

items.headline: City Council approves FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: Milpitas updated development impact fees and various user charges for the upcoming fiscal year. These fees affect housing and commercial projects in the city and determine costs for permits and services residents and developers pay.

items.headline: County homelessness plan presentation to Milpitas City Council
items.summary: Santa Clara County presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness to the Milpitas City Council. The presentation outlined regional efforts to address homelessness that may involve city resources and policies.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 housing development moves forward
items.summary: Four new homes permitted on Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development, ranging from $487k-$573k. This represents a significant residential expansion in the area with multiple unit types approved simultaneously.

items.headline: City Council Reviews Building Permitting Process and Timelines
items.summary: The council received guidance on San José's development permitting process, from initial project conception through occupancy. This presentation could clarify how long new housing, commercial, and other construction projects typically take to get approved.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates climate action plan toward 2028 goals
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on the Climate Action Playbook with a new game plan for FY 2025-26. The city is tracking its environmental commitments and adjusting strategies to meet future targets.
